mnemonics: DINR (Degradation, Inverse, Noise, Reconstruction) - The core restoration process.
Think 'Noise is Nasty, Filters are Friendly' to remember noise reduction.
HTS (Histogram, Transformation, Specification) - Steps for histogram processing.
keyPoints: Image restoration aims to recover an original image from a degraded version using a model of the degradation process.
Noise models (e.g., Gaussian, salt & pepper) are identified and reduced using spatial (mean, median) or frequency domain filters.
Reconstruction from projections, like in CT scans, uses techniques such as the Fourier Slice Theorem to build an image from its line integrals.
commonMistakes: Confusing image enhancement (subjective improvement) with restoration (objective reversal of degradation).
Applying the wrong filter for a specific noise type (e.g., using a mean filter on salt & pepper noise, which blurs the image, instead of a median filter).
Misunderstanding the role of the Point Spread Function (PSF); it's the model of the blur, not the tool to fix it.
